audit_items.proof_trace: The sole explicit decrRefCount(aux) at L429 is safe: rewriteClientCommandArgument (networking.c:2200) calls incrRefCount(newval) before setting c->argv[i], bringing aux refcount to 2 before the decrement returns it to 1—aux remains alive. All tryObjectEncoding call-sites atomically reassign c->argv[N] = tryObjectEncoding(c->argv[N]), so any internally-freed original pointer is never reachable afterward. dbOverwrite and dbUnshareStringValue correctly free old objects internally, and callers use only the newly returned objects for all subsequent access.
